entry.title: SolidFire PowerShell Tools, Python CLI and SDK pip packages v1.7

link.title: SolidFire PowerShell Tools, Python CLI and SDK pip packages v1.7

entry.summary: This is a nice Christmas present as far as I am concerned, because it’s been a while since SolidFire PowerShell tools, sfcli and their respective SDKs v1.5 have been released.

entry.title: Rancher Kubernetes Layer-7 NLB/Ingress with NGINX Plus and Let’s Encrypt

link.title: Rancher Kubernetes Layer-7 NLB/Ingress with NGINX Plus and Let's Encrypt

entry.summary: tl;dr Introduction and requirements Resources Deploy Linux VM and N+ Create DNS entries, get TLS certificates, configure NGINX Prepare DNS and open public firewall (port TCP/443) TLS with Let’s Encrypt Install Rancher Servers Configure N+ Upstream (Rancher) servers on different network kubectl X.509 ‘certificate signed by unknown authority’ error Use N+ to reverse-proxy multiple Rancher clusters or services Use N+ to reverse-proxy other API or service endpoints Next steps

entry.title: Get NetApp Hybrid Cloud Control logs via the HCC API

link.title: Get NetApp Hybrid Cloud Control logs via the HCC API

entry.summary: Hybrid Cloud Control (HCC) is a group of services - management plane, if you will - for out-of-band management of NetApp HCI, SolidFire and eSDS. Currently it’s a bunch of containers that runs in a VM commonly called mNode (short for Management Node).

entry.title: How much slower is iSCSI vs. Fibre Channel

link.title: How much slower is iSCSI vs. Fibre Channel

entry.summary: If you search for “iSCSI vs FC performance” it’s not easy to find a recent like-for-like comparison from enterprise vendors. I’ve been puzzled by this and I have some theories as to why, but regardless - the fact is there aren’t that many comparisons out there.

entry.title: Set Temporary Storage QoS Policy for SolidFire Volume

link.title: Set Temporary Storage QoS Policy for SolidFire Volume

entry.summary: Say we have a Volume ID 321 with a storage QoS Policy ID 5, and need to run a temporary job that would benefit from a different Storage QoS Policy (higher or lower, it doesn’t matter).

entry.title: SolidFire mNode and HCC Log Fowarding

link.title: SolidFire mNode and HCC Log Fowarding

entry.summary: SolidFire Management Node (aka mNode) is a VM that runs Hybrid Cloud Services (various “management stuff”.) Let’s say we wanted to collect and analyze those logs.

entry.title: SolidFire Provider for Terraform
entry.summary: The Provider is now available from the official Terraform registry. This means we no longer need to download and build it manually. It is also easy to use it from Terraform scripts because it is deployed automatically like other providers available from the registry.

entry.title: Helpful waste of time
entry.summary: When dealing with input that consists of unweildy TLS certificates Postman can helpfully hint at potential JSON validity problems and save you time. I’m sure some other tools are like that as well.

entry.title: Deploying Rancher Worker VMs to NetApp HCI with VMware VSS

link.title: Deploying Rancher Worker VMs to NetApp HCI with VMware VSS

entry.summary: Earlier this week I spotted the advice to not select a VM host when deploying Rancher worker VMs to a vSphere environment where DRS is enabled, because “DRS is smarter than you.”

entry.title: Dark Mode for NetApp HCI and SolidFire

link.title: Dark Mode for NetApp HCI and SolidFire

entry.summary: NetApp HCI, SolidFire, eSDS Web UI
